refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 percentile), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refinement adds observability (telemetry reporting) and clarifies the statistical acceptance criterion (p95, not mean), which is critical for safety-critical systems where tail latency matters.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Collect cycle time samples across ≥100 load→travel→dump cycles under defined duty cycle conditions; compute p95 percentile; compare against CT_target threshold; validate via telemetry data logs and site acceptance testing.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-operations within the cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall measure and report cycle time telemetry (start, end, duration) for each cycle.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce operational policies and safety constraints that do not degrade cycle time below CT_target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle powertrain, hydraulics, and control algorithms to minimize load, travel, and dump operation durations; implement real-time cycle time monitoring and telemetry streaming.

DP-B: Allocate cycle time budget across load, travel, and dump sub-operations; design vehicle subsystems (engine, transmission, hydraulics, bucket) to meet allocated budgets; implement cycle time measurement and reporting via telemetry.

DP-C: Implement tele-operated control with low-latency network link and predictive algorithms to reduce operator reaction time; combine with optimized vehicle dynamics to achieve CT_target; stream cycle time telemetry.

DP-D: Decompose cycle time into load, travel, and dump phases; design each phase with performance targets; implement closed-loop cycle time feedback via telemetry; use safety-aware scheduling to balance safety constraints and cycle time.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Allocate cycle time budget across load, travel, and dump sub-operations; design vehicle subsystems (engine, transmission, hydraulics, bucket) to meet allocated budgets; implement cycle time measurement and reporting via telemetry.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle powertrain design and hydraulic system responsiveness (both affect travel and dump speed).
Coupling between safety constraints (e.g., speed limiters, stability control) and achievable cycle time (safety may impose latency).
Coupling between telemetry reporting frequency and network bandwidth (high-frequency reporting may saturate Network Link).
coupling_check.notes: DP-B is weakly coupled: it separates the logical allocation of cycle time budgets (design domain) from the physical realization of each subsystem. Couplings are manageable via interface contracts (e.g., travel subsystem must achieve ≤T_travel seconds). Safety coupling is acknowledged as a risk and must be managed at the next decomposition level.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable given site conditions (soil, slope, load mass) and safety constraints. Risk: If CT_target is unrealistic, no DP will succeed; recommend early validation with customer and site data.

Assumption: Vehicle subsystems (engine, transmission, hydraulics) can be designed/tuned to meet allocated budgets independently. Risk: Cross-subsystem interactions (e.g., engine load affecting transmission shift timing) may violate independence; recommend coupled analysis.

Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure (Network Link, Tasking interface) has sufficient bandwidth and latency to stream cycle time data without impacting vehicle control. Risk: Network congestion or latency spikes may corrupt telemetry or introduce control delays; recommend network QoS design.

Assumption: Safety constraints do not impose cycle time penalties that push p95 above CT_target. Risk: Safety-critical checks (e.g., stability verification before dump) may add latency; recommend early safety-performance trade-off analysis.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly state the cycle time budget allocation targets for load, travel, and dump phases (e.g., T_load ≤ 15s, T_travel ≤ 45s, T_dump ≤ 10s, total ≤ 70s). Add a clause requiring safety-performance trade-off analysis to confirm that safety constraints do not violate the allocated budgets. Clarify telemetry reporting cadence and network QoS requirements.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: The Vehicle module owns the physical realization of load, travel, and dump sub-operations, including powertrain (engine, transmission), hydraulic system (pump, cylinders, valves), bucket mechanism, and onboard sensors (cycle time measurement). The Vehicle module is responsible for meeting the allocated cycle time budgets for each sub-operation and reporting cycle time telemetry via the Telemetry interface.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle streams cycle time measurements (operation start timestamp, end timestamp, duration, phase identifier) to the NGHE Solution for aggregation, analysis, and reporting to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives operational commands (load, travel, dump) from the NGHE Solution (or Teleoperation module in tele-operated mode) and executes them within the allocated cycle time budgets.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives safety constraints and alerts (e.g., speed limits, stability warnings, emergency stop) from the Safety Services module and enforces them during operation, even if they impact cycle time.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system integrator and would own cycle time KPI monitoring and reporting, but the physical realization of cycle time performance (vehicle dynamics, subsystem responsiveness) must reside in Vehicle. Assigning IM to NGHE Solution would diffuse the cycle time design responsibility across multiple modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), violating cohesion. Not recommended.

Vehicle is the primary physical realization of cycle time performance. It owns powertrain, hydraulics, bucket, and onboard sensors. Cycle time measurement and telemetry reporting are tightly coupled to vehicle operation and should be contained within Vehicle. High containment, low diffusion. Recommended.

Teleoperation module handles remote operator control and network communication, but does not own the physical vehicle subsystems that determine cycle time performance. Assigning IM to Teleoperation would create diffusion: cycle time design would be split between Teleoperation (control logic) and Vehicle (physical realization). Not recommended.

Safety Services owns safety constraints and alerts, but not the physical realization of cycle time performance. Assigning IM to Safety Services would create high diffusion and violate separation of concerns. Not recommended.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Vehicle module contains the physical subsystems (powertrain, hydraulics, bucket, sensors) that directly determine cycle time performance. Cycle time measurement and telemetry reporting are integral to vehicle operation and should be owned by Vehicle. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) define the contract between Vehicle and the rest of the NGHE Solution, allowing Vehicle to operate autonomously within its allocated cycle time budgets while respecting safety constraints.
containment_risks: Risk: Cycle time measurement logic (e.g., timestamp capture, duration calculation) may be duplicated across Vehicle and NGHE Solution if not carefully managed. Mitigation: Define a single source of truth for cycle time measurement (Vehicle) and ensure NGHE Solution only aggregates and reports data from Vehicle telemetry.

Risk: Safety constraints (SafetyAlert) may impose latency that violates cycle time budgets. Mitigation: Require Safety Services to quantify the latency impact of each safety constraint and validate that the total latency does not exceed the allocated cycle time budget.

Risk: Network latency or packet loss on Telemetry interface may corrupt cycle time data or delay reporting, affecting KPI verification. Mitigation: Implement redundant telemetry channels, checksums, and timestamping at the Vehicle level to ensure data integrity.

Risk: Teleoperation mode may introduce operator reaction time that increases cycle time beyond the Vehicle's physical capability. Mitigation: Allocate a portion of the cycle time budget to operator reaction time and validate that the remaining budget is achievable by Vehicle subsystems.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the cycle time measurement mechanism: specify whether timestamps are captured by Vehicle onboard sensors (preferred for accuracy) or by NGHE Solution based on Tasking commands and Telemetry reports (less accurate due to network latency). Define the telemetry reporting cadence (e.g., one report per cycle, or continuous streaming) and specify the data schema (operation phase, start time, end time, duration, status). Establish a contract between Vehicle and Safety Services that quantifies the maximum latency imposed by safety constraints and confirms that the total cycle time (including safety latency) does not exceed CT_target.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (cycle time budget allocation and subsystem design) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target). The DP is logically sound: decomposing cycle time into load, travel, and dump phases and allocating budgets to each phase is a standard approach in performance engineering. The DP includes telemetry measurement and reporting, which enables verification of the FR. No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: If cycle time measurement logic is implemented in both Vehicle and NGHE Solution, the two implementations may diverge, leading to inconsistent KPI reporting. Mitigation: Implement cycle time measurement once in Vehicle and have NGHE Solution aggregate telemetry data without re-computing cycle time.

Diffusion Risk 2: If safety constraints are not tightly integrated with Vehicle subsystem design, safety-imposed latency may not be accounted for in the cycle time budget. Mitigation: Require Safety Services to provide latency specifications for each safety constraint, and Vehicle to validate that the total latency fits within the allocated budget.

Diffusion Risk 3: If telemetry reporting is not carefully designed, network congestion may delay cycle time data, affecting real-time KPI monitoring. Mitigation: Implement local cycle time buffering in Vehicle and asynchronous telemetry streaming to decouple vehicle operation from network latency.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module is the clear owner of cycle time performance realization. Interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) are well-defined and minimize coupling. Telemetry interface is the primary external dependency; network QoS must be managed at the NGHE Solution level. SafetyAlert interface introduces a constraint that must be validated during subsystem design.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via site acceptance testing: collect cycle time samples across ≥100 cycles under defined duty cycle conditions, compute p95 percentile, and compare against CT_target. Telemetry data from Vehicle provides the measurement source. Verification requires access to site conditions, load mass, and soil properties to ensure representativeness.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Vehicle is responsible for achieving the allocated cycle time budgets for load, travel, and dump phases. NGHE Solution is responsible for aggregating telemetry data and reporting KPI to stakeholders. Safety Services is responsible for providing safety constraints that do not violate the cycle time budget. Ownership boundaries are well-defined.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Refine the cycle time budget allocation targets (T_load, T_travel, T_dump) based on customer requirements and site conditions. Validate that the sum of allocated budgets plus safety latency does not exceed CT_target.

Revision 2: Define the telemetry reporting schema and cadence (e.g., one report per cycle, JSON format, 100ms latency SLA). Specify the network QoS requirements (bandwidth, latency, reliability) to ensure telemetry does not impact vehicle control.

Revision 3: Establish a contract between Vehicle and Safety Services that quantifies the maximum latency imposed by each safety constraint. Validate that the total safety latency is acceptable and does not violate the cycle time budget.

Revision 4: Define the measurement methodology for cycle time (e.g., timestamp capture at Vehicle level, phase identification logic, duration calculation). Specify the accuracy and precision requirements (e.g., ±100ms).

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-defined and the selected DP is logically sound. The assigned IM (Vehicle) is the correct ownership boundary with high containment and low diffusion. However, the DP requires further decomposition to address the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-002.1, FR-NGHE-002.2, FR-NGHE-002.3) and to resolve the identified risks and assumptions. Specifically: (1) the cycle time budget allocation targets (T_load, T_travel, T_dump) must be derived from CT_target and validated against site conditions; (2) the telemetry measurement and reporting mechanism must be designed to ensure accuracy and minimize network impact; (3) the safety-performance trade-off must be analyzed to confirm that safety constraints do not violate the cycle time budget. These tasks require decomposition to the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-operations within the cycle time budget (T_load ≤ T_load_target, T_travel ≤ T_travel_target, T_dump ≤ T_dump_target).
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall measure and report cycle time telemetry (operation phase, start timestamp, end timestamp, duration) with ±100ms accuracy and <100ms latency.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, stability checks, emergency stop) without violating the allocated cycle time budgets.

assumptions: Assumption 1: CT_target is achievable given site conditions (soil, slope, load mass) and safety constraints. This assumption must be validated with customer and site data before detailed design.

Assumption 2: Vehicle subsystems (engine, transmission, hydraulics) can be designed/tuned to meet allocated cycle time budgets independently. This assumption must be validated via coupled analysis of powertrain and hydraulic interactions.

Assumption 3: Telemetry infrastructure (Network Link, Tasking interface) has sufficient bandwidth and latency to stream cycle time data without impacting vehicle control. This assumption must be validated via network QoS analysis.
Assumption 4: Safety constraints do not impose cycle time penalties that push p95 above CT_target. This assumption must be validated via safety-performance trade-off analysis.
Assumption 5: Cycle time measurement can be implemented with ±100ms accuracy using onboard Vehicle sensors. This assumption must be validated via sensor selection and calibration.
open_risks: Risk 1 (High): CT_target may be unrealistic given site conditions and safety constraints. Mitigation: Conduct early feasibility study with customer and site data.
Risk 2 (High): Safety constraints may impose latency that violates cycle time budgets. Mitigation: Require Safety Services to quantify latency impact and validate total cycle time.

Risk 3 (Medium): Cycle time measurement logic may be duplicated across Vehicle and NGHE Solution, leading to inconsistency. Mitigation: Implement single source of truth in Vehicle; NGHE Solution aggregates only.
Risk 4 (Medium): Network latency or packet loss may corrupt telemetry data or delay KPI reporting. Mitigation: Implement redundant telemetry channels, checksums, and local buffering in Vehicle.

Risk 5 (Medium): Teleoperation mode may introduce operator reaction time that increases cycle time beyond Vehicle's physical capability. Mitigation: Allocate portion of cycle time budget to operator reaction time; validate remaining budget is achievable.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Site conditions data (soil type, slope, load mass, ambient temperature) to validate CT_target feasibility.
Evidence 2: Vehicle subsystem performance specifications (engine power, transmission shift time, hydraulic flow rate, bucket cycle time) to validate cycle time budget allocation.
Evidence 3: Safety constraint latency specifications (e.g., stability check latency, emergency stop response time) to validate safety-performance trade-off.
Evidence 4: Network QoS analysis (bandwidth, latency, reliability) to validate telemetry infrastructure adequacy.
Evidence 5: Sensor selection and calibration data to validate cycle time measurement accuracy (±100ms).
Evidence 6: Site acceptance test plan and results (≥100 cycle samples, p95 percentile calculation, comparison against CT_target).
